name: Goldman Sachs BDC, Inc.
business: Goldman Sachs BDC, Inc. is a specialty finance company focused on lending to middle-market companies. It originates secured debt (first lien, unitranche, second lien), unsecured debt (mezzanine), and select equity investments, targeting current income and capital appreciation. The company has elected to be regulated as a business development company (BDC) and as a regulated investment company (RIC).
sellsTo: Opportunistic investments in large U.S. companies, foreign companies, stressed or distressed debt, structured products

exposures.sentence: The largest industries in our portfolio, based on fair value as of December 31, 2025, were Software, Health Care Providers & Services, Health Care Technology, which represented 17.6%, 8.8% and 8.4%, respectively, of our portfolio at fair value.
the impact of information technology system failures, data security breaches, data privacy compliance, network disruptions, and cybersecurity attacks

catalysts.event: Repayment of 2026 Notes using Revolving Credit Facility proceeds
catalysts.sentence: On January 15, 2026, we borrowed $505.00 million under the Revolving Credit Facility and used the proceeds, together with cash on hand, to repay the 2026 Notes, plus accrued and unpaid interest, in full satisfaction of our obligations under the 2026 Notes.
The 2027 Notes will mature on March 11, 2027 and may be redeemed in whole or in part at our option at any time or from time to time at the redemption prices set forth in the indenture.
The 2030 Notes will mature on September 9, 2030 and may be redeemed in whole or in part at our option at any time or from time to time at the redemption prices set forth in the indenture.

On June 13, 2025, we entered into the 2025 10b5-1 Plan with Georgeson Securities Corporation (Georgeson) for repurchases of our common stock during the period from June 16, 2025 through June 13, 2026.
On and effective June 5, 2025, we terminated the 2023 Equity Distribution Agreement in accordance with its terms.

catalysts.event: Grant of co-investment exemptive relief by SEC
catalysts.sentence: On May 21, 2025, the SEC granted the Relief (the Relief) to our Investment Adviser, the BDCs advised by our Investment Adviser and certain other affiliated applicants, which superseded the prior co-investment exemptive relief received on November 16, 2022, as amended on June 25, 2024 (the Prior Relief).
On September 9, 2025, we closed an offering of $400.00 million aggregate principal amount of 5.650% unsecured notes due 2030 (the 2030 Notes).

tamClaims.claim: U.S. middle market comprises nearly 200,000 companies representing approximately 33% of private sector GDP.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the National Center for the Middle Market, the U.S. middle market is composed of nearly 200,000 companies that represent approximately 33% of the private sector gross domestic product.
